root_cause: tournament.py 'from networkx... import *' bound nx's @_dispatchable functions; called on an fnx graph they routed through nx's backend-dispatch machinery (~2.5ms pure overhead). For trivial score_sequence (sorted out-degrees) that is 280x; for is_tournament it adds to nx's O(V^2) all-pairs edge check on the slow fnx adjacency view (6x).
lever: override both in tournament.py, bypassing the dispatcher: score_sequence = sorted(G.out_degree()) directly; is_tournament snapshots successor adjacency to plain set dicts then runs nx's exact XOR-over-pairs test. Exception order fixed to match nx (multigraph checked before undirected). Deterministic outputs, byte-exact.

parity.coverage: tournaments (score_sequence + is_tournament), gnp non-tournaments (25 False), self-loop, MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph/Graph exception-message parity

headline: tournament.score_sequence 280x slower than nx -> PARITY (~276x self-speedup); is_tournament 6x slower -> 5.4x FASTER than nx (~32x self-speedup). Cause was the nx @_dispatchable backend-dispatch overhead on import*-re-exported functions. Pure Python, no rebuild.
